chunk_summary: This section outlines role mapping for frontline workers in India's nutrition programs, emphasizing clarity in responsibilities, clinic instructions for pediatricians, and integration of protocols for IFA, deworming, and malnutrition management.
learning_objectives: Understand the roles of AWW, ASHA, ANM, and school staff in nutrition program delivery.
Learn clinic documentation protocols for pediatricians managing IFA and malnutrition cases.
Identify adverse event management strategies for deworming and vitamin A supplementation.
key_facts: Anganwadi workers (AWW) handle supplementary nutrition distribution, growth monitoring, and counseling.
ASHA workers focus on mobilization, home follow-up, and linking families to health sessions.
Teachers and school health staff deliver weekly IFA and biannual deworming doses under program protocols.
Pediatricians must pause weekly IFA during therapeutic iron dosing to avoid duplication.
Severe acute malnutrition cases require referral to nutrition rehabilitation services with stabilization protocols.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Teachers and school health staff deliver weekly IFA and biannual deworming doses under programme protocols, with the nearest health facility designated for adverse event triage. (National Deworming Day Operational Guidelines)
document_key_topics: Role mapping for frontline workers in nutrition programs
Integration of IFA, deworming, and malnutrition protocols
